epigraph.text: For every problem there is a solution that is simple, neat, and wrong.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Lawyer Sues Gravity for $8 Million in Damages
follyOfTheDay.summary:
A personal injury attorney in Albuquerque filed suit against gravity itself, alleging negligent design and reckless disregard for human safety. The complaint, spanning forty-seven pages and citing Newton's work as evidence of the defendant's "dangerous and outdated" operational model, seeks damages including medical expenses, lost wages, and punitive damages for "persistent and malicious downward acceleration."

The court has not yet determined whether to serve the defendant, though legal scholars note that gravity's presence everywhere may constitute adequate notice. A spokesperson for the defendant—if gravity could be said to have a spokesperson—could not be reached for comment.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: When Nature becomes a tort defendant, the judicial system has achieved true comedy.
